title: Modified Nasal Swab For Detection of Sars-Cov2
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dih1o","text":"The common methods of sample collection for the detection of SARS-Cov2 are either pharyngeal or nasopharyngeal. Both these methods require specific swabs that are in short supply, and both methods are not easily self-administered. A nasal swab can also be used to collect samples and is relatively easy to self-administer, yet obtaining nasal specific swabs can now also be a challenge. In order to address these issues, our group has optimized a protocol for self-administered nasal collection using buccal swabs that are not currently in high demand. With this method, we have been able to collect human samples with reliable results in RNA extraction based on RNaseP, and can detect SARS-Cov2 in known positive cases of COVID-19. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f2hqs","text":"Be sure all materials are present prior to collection and all of the paperwork is in order. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"514ri","text":"Appropriate PPE must be worn while at the collection site. Care must be taken to maintain distance between the individual collecting the sample and the individual self-swabbing. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"13d55","text":"Swabs (Fisher, NC9679349) NOTE: We substitute buccal swabs for nasal swabs as they are not in high demand","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c5p6f","text":"Spray bottle with 70% ethanol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"13ltm","text":"Biohazard bags (autoclavable plastic)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"augog","text":"Cooler","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2dsks","text":"Ice packs","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transport the cooler and swabbing kit to the site for sample processing and place in a 4ºC fridge or coldroom
Place the biohazard bag in the cooler on top of cooler packs
Decontaminate the exterior of the biohazard bag with 70% ethanol.
Place the tubes in biohazard bag.
Holding the lid shut, pull straight on the handle to remove stick through the opening and leave swab pad in tube. Insert plug securely. Discard stick.
Remove swab, slide into tube and secure lid.
Insert swab gently into your nostril until you meet some resistance. Gently scrub all sides of the nostril and then leave the swab in place for 15 seconds. Repeat in the other nostril
Pick up swab the sample collecter has delivered. Remove swab from its sleeve. Open sleeve the whole way and lay the swab on the opened sleeve.
Wash your hands with soap and water.
Pick up swabbing kit and transport to the collection site (kit contains buccal swab, cooler, ice packs, spray bottle with 70% ethanol, biohazard bags, and necessary paperwork).
Take the swab out of the kit, place it on a clean surface, and back away so the individual self-swabbing can pick up the swab.
Establish contact with the individual to be swabbed and provide instructions.
Place the biohazard on a clean surface and back away.
Remove PPE before returning to your vehicle.
creator: Joseph Patterson
